metricsSummary: Season context: SS26 week 11 of 26. Roughly 3.3 months remain in the selling season. Overall: revenue $244,882, gross margin 46%, blended sell-through 26%, inventory at cost $365,328, inventory at retail $922,409, annualized turns 1.7x. Top 10 brands by revenue: ICHI ($62,314, ST 23%, GM 63%, sold 1,119, stock 3,685); DAZE ($33,688, ST 29%, GM 56%, sold 236, stock 572); Z SUPPLY ($30,953, ST 41%, GM 56%, sold 328, stock 466); RD STYLE ($26,847, ST 31%, GM 62%, sold 415, stock 921); B YOUNG ($26,042, ST 21%, GM 63%, sold 549, stock 2,043); PILGRIM ($17,689, ST 28%, GM 59%, sold 384, stock 1,003); SAINT TROPEZ ($13,145, ST 22%, GM 60%, sold 190, stock 664); DEX ($9,138, ST 31%, GM 61%, sold 137, stock 299); DELUC ($7,850, ST 30%, GM 58%, sold 112, stock 258); SADIE AND SAGE ($4,250, ST 21%, GM 57%, sold 72, stock 268). Bottom 5 brands by sell-through: GIRL DANGER (2%, $89, stock 40); GLAMOROUS (9%, $80, stock 43); GENTLE FAWN (10%, $20, stock 9); CAMILLE (11%, $33, stock 8); COLAB (13%, $998, stock 67). Categories: TOP (ST 22%, WOS 39.0, stock 4,195, revenue $58,190); DENIM (ST 30%, WOS 25.0, stock 771, revenue $40,443); SWEATER (ST 40%, WOS 17.0, stock 832, revenue $33,551); PANT (ST 26%, WOS 31.0, stock 995, revenue $25,334); JACKET (ST 42%, WOS 15.0, stock 258, revenue $21,589); JEWELRY (ST 27%, WOS 29.0, stock 902, revenue $16,016); CARDIGAN (ST 27%, WOS 30.0, stock 521, revenue $12,655); DRESS (ST 13%, WOS 72.0, stock 712, revenue $6,654); BLAZER (ST 21%, WOS 42.0, stock 252, revenue $6,362); ACCESSORIES (ST 19%, WOS 48.0, stock 714, revenue $5,291); VEST (ST 49%, WOS 11.0, stock 70, revenue $2,860); SKIRT (ST 25%, WOS 32.0, stock 171, revenue $2,748). Reorder alerts count: 22. Reorder-now styles: ICHI — ETERNITY STRIPE KNIT TOP (NAVY) (1.8/wk, 84%); ICHI — MULTICOLORED KNIT TOP (6.0/wk, 48%); ICHI — CORNSTALK JACKET (0.8/wk, 100%); B YOUNG — LOOSE FIT VNECK KNIT TOP (5.4/wk, 41%); B YOUNG — LYDIA WHITE POCKET JEANS (1.5/wk, 57%); B YOUNG — WHITE CARDIGAN WITH TORTOISE SHELL BUTTONS (1.5/wk, 67%); B YOUNG — WHITE LIGHTWEIGHT TEE (0.7/wk, 83%); B YOUNG — BLUE LIGHTWEIGHT TEE (0.7/wk, 71%); B YOUNG — ABSOLUTE NAVY STRIPE SHORT SLEEVE SHIRT (1.1/wk, 50%); z supply — SCOUT LINEN PANT (CHOCOLATE) (7.3/wk, 79%). Top sold-out styles by velocity: Unknown Gift Card (3.2/wk, 100%, 469.5 weeks); Freight moncton (2.8/wk, 100%, 68.8 weeks); RD STYLE SELBY FUNNEL NECK VEST (2.7/wk, 100%, 27.1 weeks); z supply ALPS HALF ZIP SWEATER (2.4/wk, 100%, 23.4 weeks); RD STYLE SOFT KNIT BROWN HALF ZIP (2.4/wk, 100%, 15.9 weeks). Size gaps under-bought: M (+6 pts; demand 28%, buy 22%); 27 (+4 pts; demand 18%, buy 14%); 30 (+4 pts; demand 11%, buy 7%); L (+3 pts; demand 17%, buy 14%). Size gaps over-bought: XXL (-4 pts; demand 2%, buy 6%); 26 (-4 pts; demand 14%, buy 18%); XL (-3 pts; demand 8%, buy 11%); 28 (-3 pts; demand 13%, buy 16%). Store breakdown: Saint John: $109,836 revenue, 43%, 1,845 units, AOV $136; Moncton: $79,419 revenue, 50%, 1,158 units, AOV $165; Halifax: $55,627 revenue, 48%, 846 units, AOV $127. Best performers: RD STYLE — SOFT KNIT BROWN HALF ZIP (9 units, $711); PILGRIM — GIFT WITH PURCHASE (RED HEART - GOLD) (9 units, $0); RD STYLE — DARK GREY CREW NECK PULLOVER (8 units, $316); PILGRIM — ARASH GOLD TENNIS BRACELET (8 units, $232); PILGRIM — INDIE BRACELET (DEEP RED) (7 units, $90); ICHI — ETERNITY STRIPE KNIT TOP (GREEN) (7 units, $404); RD STYLE — KERWIN POPLIN HI-LOW SHIRT (WHITE) (7 units, $452); RD STYLE — VICTORIA BLACK PULL ON SWEATS (6 units, $474). Slow movers: ICHI — DAHLIA SKINNY BROWN BELT (1 units, $23, 0.1/wk); PILGRIM — JAMESON SILVER RING (1 units, $59, 0.1/wk); PILGRIM — SPARKLE GOLD NECKLACE SET (1 units, $69, 0.1/wk); ICHI — DASILA CARDIGAN (BLUE) (1 units, $69, 0.1/wk); B YOUNG — FUZZY CONTRAST SWEATER (1 units, $55, 0.1/wk); Z SUPPLY — SCOUT COTTON JERSEY PANT (GREEN) (1 units, $53, 0.1/wk); Z SUPPLY — ALL DAY KNIT JACKET (CHOCOLATE) (1 units, $101, 0.1/wk); LA — TIE FRONT KNIT CARDI (CREAM) (1 units, $48, 0.1/wk). Customers: total 13,357, with orders 2,242, total spent $658,426. Top customers: Alissa DeWare ($0, 32 orders); Alison Bulmer ($0, 16 orders); Alexandra Woodworth ($0, 47 orders); Linda Boudreau ($0, 29 orders); Karen Thomas ($0, 31 orders). Channels: 30d revenue $63,172 with POS $58,672 and online $4,500.
seasonContext: SS26 is in week 11 of 26 with about 3.3 months left. It is still early enough that March-to-June acceleration matters, so decisions should focus on clean reorders in proven winners and tighter control on slow categories.

briefing.morningGreeting: Good morning — revenue is $244,882 so far this season, with ICHI leading the brand stack. Focus today on protecting winners and clearing capital from slow inventory.
briefing.healthContext: The dashboard is carrying meaningful inventory while spring sell-through is still early. That makes turns and weeks of supply look heavy now, especially in TOP, but the right move is selective reorders plus faster action on dead stock.

salesAnalytics.revenueInsight: ICHI and TOP are driving the top line right now. Push what is already converting instead of spreading buys too wide across weak brands.
salesAnalytics.brandInsight: ICHI deserves close review for reorder depth, while the lowest sell-through brands should be cut back or marked down. Keep brand decisions simple: grow proven demand, reduce stuck inventory.
salesAnalytics.channelInsight: All revenue flows through Lightspeed POS across 3 stores. Focus markdown events and promotional pushes on in-store traffic and email marketing.

sellThrough.overallInsight: Current sell-through is early-season and should not be judged like late May. Reorder proven winners fast, but do not overreact by chasing every small sample.
sellThrough.topPerformers: Unknown Gift Card is already telling you where demand is real. Rebuy into proven velocity and keep urbanbutter priority brands visible when they are earning it.
sellThrough.slowMovers: Review the slowest brands and categories now, not at season end. Mark down or transfer anything with weak velocity before it becomes carryover.
sellThrough.brandRecommendations: Grow the brands with strong revenue, clean margin, and real velocity. Maintain middle performers, and reduce or clear brands sitting on too much stock for their current sell-through.

inventoryHealth.agingInsight: Aging inventory is the main risk because tied-up capital will block opportunistic reorders. Pull a clean list of 60+ day product and decide this week what gets marked down, transferred, or held.
inventoryHealth.categoryWOS: Watch weeks of supply closely in categories with weak sell-through. Keep future receipts focused on the categories already proving demand.
inventoryHealth.sizeGaps: Use size data to fix buy shape, not just total units. Add depth where demand is outrunning buys and trim sizes that keep stacking up.

buyPlanning.otbContext: Open-to-buy should stay focused on categories and brands already earning their way back in. With 3.3 months left, the job is to stay flexible enough to reorder winners without deepening weak inventory.
buyPlanning.seasonalComparison: SS25 was a 2-store base and SS26 is now 3 stores, so raw volume is not a clean comparison on its own. Judge this season by sell-through quality, margin, and whether winners are scaling cleanly store to store.
buyPlanning.actionItems: 1) Reorder the top proven winners, especially any sold-out styles with real weekly velocity. 2) Build a markdown list for the slowest brands and 60+ day inventory. 3) Adjust future receipts toward the sizes and categories already showing demand.

customers.vipInsight: Top customers are worth protecting with early access to new winners and personal follow-up. Keep your best spenders close to the strongest new deliveries.
customers.segmentInsight: Customer mix should guide how aggressive you are with reorders versus clearance. Lean on loyal repeat shoppers to move high-conviction product first.
